words.definition: the solving of a problem or ending of a conflict
the state of being better or greater than others
relating to the universe or outer space; very large
a violent storm with strong winds
a plan of action designed to achieve a goal
done by choice, without being forced
words.sentence: From The Power of Gentle Persuasion

words.definition: the act of convincing someone through reasoning
words.sentence: From The Power of Gentle Persuasion

questions.question: What were the Wind and the Sun arguing about?
options: Which of them was stronger and more powerful

questions.question: How did the Wind show his power?
options: By uprooting trees, tearing roofs, and moving ships

questions.question: What did the Sun suggest to settle their argument?
options: To have a contest that would demonstrate their power
To ask other forces of nature to judge

questions.question: What was the specific challenge they agreed upon?
options: To see who could create the biggest storm
To see who could make a traveler remove his cloak
To see who could move the heaviest object

questions.question: How did the traveler respond to the Wind's powerful gusts?
options: He pulled his cloak more tightly around himself

questions.question: What happened as the Wind blew harder and harder?
options: The man clutched his cloak even more tightly

questions.question: How did the Wind feel about his lack of success?
What happened when the Wind used even more power?
options: The traveler finally removed his cloak
The man wrapped his cloak even more securely

questions.question: How was the Sun's approach different from the Wind's?
options: The Sun used even more force
The Sun used gentle warmth instead of force and violence

questions.question: How did the traveler respond to the Sun's warmth?
options: He began to relax and feel comfortable

questions.question: What did the traveler do when he became too warm?
What lesson did the Wind learn from this contest?
options: That he should blow even harder
That gentleness and persuasion can be more powerful than force

informationalTexts.title: Coral Reefs
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) tiny marine animals called coral polyps
2. a) they support biodiversity and protect coastlines

informationalTexts.title: Architects
informationalTexts.content:
1. a) safety, beauty, cost, and how the building will be used
2. a) engineers, contractors, and other specialists

grammarLessons.content:
**Nouns**

This lesson focuses on Nouns. As you read today's story, pay attention to examples of this concept.

**Key Points:**
• Understand the concept
• Find examples in the story
• Practice using it correctly

**Practice:**
Apply what you learned by completing the exercises.

**Remember:** Nouns is an important language skill!
grammarLessons.objective: Students will understand and apply Nouns.

languageLessons.content:
**ABC Order**

This language lesson teaches ABC Order. Look for examples in today's story.

**Key Ideas:**
• Learn the concept
• Find examples
• Practice the skill

**Practice Activities:**
Complete the exercises to master this skill.

**Remember:** Practice makes perfect!
languageLessons.objective: Students will learn about ABC Order.

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
